Title: Cotton metabolism regulatory network: Unraveling key genes and pathways in fiber development and growth regulation

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) elongation; fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation in developing cotton fibers
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Rate-limiting step; upstream/committed condensation step of VLCFA elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-ketoacyl-CoA elongated acyl-CoA intermediates leading to C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0 saturated VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/directly promotes target VLCFA pool required for fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; KCS is stated as the rate-limiting enzyme for fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZM24; pag1 mutant; transgenic ZM24 lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in ZM24 fibers, especially at 5 and 10 DPA; previously highest at 10 DPA then decreased by 20-25 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression decreased significantly in pag1 fibers at 5 DPA and 10 DPA relative to ZM24; fiber-specific differential expression across 5, 10, and 20 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (especially C22:0, C24:0, C26:0)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fibers (10 DPA measured for VLCFAs)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cotton fiber elongation and development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes saturated VLCFA accumulation and fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS/3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase; condensing enzyme of fatty acid elongase complex
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BR-deficient pag1 background; in vitro ovule culture with exogenous C24:0 partially rescued RNAi short-fiber phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (UHPLC-QTOF-MS); transcriptomics (RNA-seq); co-expression network/WGCNA; fatty acid GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis between ZM24 and pag1; qRT-PCR; transgenic overexpression; RNAi knockdown; fatty acid profiling by GC-MS; mature fiber length measurement; in vitro ovule culture rescue with C24:0
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance cotton fiber length through transgenic overexpression or pathway engineering to increase VLCFA biosynthesis; could be combined with BR-related regulators
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS1b_Dt was identified from the cotton metabolism regulatory network as a key DEG in the VLCFA synthesis pathway of fibers. 2. Its expression was significantly reduced in BR-deficient pag1 fibers at 5 and 10 DPA versus ZM24. 3. Overexpression increased C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0 levels and increased fiber length by 7.97%, whereas RNAi reduced these VLCFAs and decreased fiber length by 6.78%. 4. Exogenous C24:0 partially rescued the short-fiber phenotype of RNAi lines, supporting a causal role through VLCFA production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The promoter contains BES1-binding G-box-related elements, suggesting possible BR-responsive regulation, but direct regulation by GhBES1.4 was proposed for future validation rather than proven here.
